{
  "weights": {
    "R1.1": 0.7,
    "R1.2": 1.0,
    "R1.3": 1.0,
    "R1.4": 0.7,
    "R1.5": 0.3,
    "R1.6": 0.3,
    "R1.7": 0.3,
    "RX.1": 0.7,
    "RX.2": 1.0,
    "RX.3": 0.7,
    "RX.4": 0.3,
    "RX.5": 0.7
  },
  "active_set": [
    "R1.1",
    "R1.2",
    "R1.3",
    "R1.4",
    "RX.1",
    "RX.2",
    "RX.3",
    "RX.4",
    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": false,
  "clarification_question": "",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Ensuring the thesis and contribution are framed clearly and defensibly is central to judging whether the work is solid.",
    "R1.2": "Internal logical coherence from method definition to stated claims must be airtight for the work to be solid.",
    "R1.3": "Comparative performance and convergence claims must be aligned with and supported by the reported experimental evidence.",
    "R1.4": "Because the text asserts a new solver and positions it against PCG, assessing the delta versus standard methods is essential.",
    "RX.1": "Clear acknowledgment of assumptions, limitations, and uncertainty strengthens the credibility of the claims.",
    "RX.2": "Avoiding overstated or overly general claims is critical to maintaining a solid, defensible presentation.",
    "RX.3": "Claims should remain within the scope of the stated problem setting (e.g., SPD, sparsity, chosen benchmarks).",
    "RX.4": "Even in a low-risk domain, care should be taken to avoid downstream misinterpretation or misuse of the results.",
    "RX.5": "Reproducible and traceable descriptions of methods and experiments are necessary to substantiate the claims."
  }
}

rationale.R1.1: Ensuring the thesis and contribution are framed clearly and defensibly is central to judging whether the work is solid.
rationale.R1.2: Internal logical coherence from method definition to stated claims must be airtight for the work to be solid.
rationale.R1.3: Comparative performance and convergence claims must be aligned with and supported by the reported experimental evidence.
rationale.R1.4: Because the text asserts a new solver and positions it against PCG, assessing the delta versus standard methods is essential.
rationale.RX.1: Clear acknowledgment of assumptions, limitations, and uncertainty strengthens the credibility of the claims.
rationale.RX.2: Avoiding overstated or overly general claims is critical to maintaining a solid, defensible presentation.
rationale.RX.3: Claims should remain within the scope of the stated problem setting (e.g., SPD, sparsity, chosen benchmarks).
rationale.RX.4: Even in a low-risk domain, care should be taken to avoid downstream misinterpretation or misuse of the results.
rationale.RX.5: Reproducible and traceable descriptions of methods and experiments are necessary to substantiate the claims.
